title: brain source trust revoke <sourceId> [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged revocation of a source's trust. The broker challenge is bound to sourceId + the source's rawContentHash; on authorization, the broker advances the trust-ledger ref to untrusted and an immutable audit record is written. Fan-out: pre-integration runs that depend on the source are moved to failed@<checkpoint> with reason trust-revoked; already-integrated runs spawn a Tier-3 remediation run. Authorized ONLY by an OS-presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op source trust revoke) or the non-interactive --export-challenge → sign → --authorization flow (security-broker-contract.md §7); --yes never authorizes. Without an authorization, exits 6 (action-required) and, with --export-challenge, emits an AuthorizationChallenge (security-broker-contract.md §7.1) instead of this success object.

rawContentHash.description: The source's raw content hash the challenge was bound to.

trustLedgerHead.description: The trust-ledger ref head after the advance.

remediationRuns.description: Tier-3 remediation run ids spawned for already-integrated dependent runs.

failedRuns.description: Pre-integration dependent run ids moved to failed@<checkpoint> with reason trust-revoked.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized (sourceId + rawContentHash-bound) revocation of a source's trust; pre-integration dependents ⇒ failed@<checkpoint>, integrated dependents ⇒ Tier-3 remediation runs.

args.description: The source to revoke trust from.

flags.description: Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op source trust revoke, bound to sourceId + rawContentHash) to stdout and exit 6 instead of revoking; sign it out of band with an enrolled approver key.
Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse produced from a prior --export-challenge; presents it to the broker to authorize the revocation.

Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat revoke with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing trust-ledger head + fan-out rather than re-revoking (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: broker advances the trust-ledger ref to untrusted
moves pre-integration dependent runs to failed@<checkpoint> (reason trust-revoked)
spawns Tier-3 remediation runs for already-integrated dependent runs
prohibitedEffects: no vault content mutation (only the trust ledger + audit record + dependent-run state)
--yes never authorizes; never bypasses the broker challenge

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <sourceId>, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
broker drift: current trust level differs from the challenge fromLevel (security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is not permitted for this op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
